response.original_title: The Internet Was Weeks Away From Disaster and No One Knew
response.overview: This video recounts the near-catastrophic XZ hack, detailing how a subtle vulnerability in a widely used compression tool almost compromised global internet security. It traces the history of the open-source movement, explains the sophisticated nature of the attack orchestrated by the mysterious Jia Tan, and highlights the accidental discovery that prevented widespread disaster, underscoring the critical need for vigilance in the open-source ecosystem.
response.tagline: The hidden flaw that almost broke the internet.
response.title: The Internet Was Weeks Away From Disaster and No One Knew
